collapse_locations.note: collapses spread across the litter zone leaning DEEPER (skirt), median x 4.45; hforce ~89>80 gate. v=0.8 COMMAND still yields stride speed bursts >1.2 that trip the collapse gate on hard contact.

wedged.note: wedged eps thrash ~14 unstick nudges and never exit; unstick recovery only 21.6% overall.

diagnosis.dominant_failure_mode: in-corridor loss BEFORE handoff (72% of failures): collapse 37.5% + wedged 25% + fall-in-litter 15.6%. Collapse is the single largest category. Only 22% of failures are post-handoff.
diagnosis.primary_cause: The FIXED middle-crawl strategy at v=0.8 is the root. (1) It is not contact-aware: the walker stride produces instantaneous planar-speed bursts >1.2 that trip the (calibrated) collapse gate on hard litter contact (hforce ~89>80) -> 37.5% collapse. (2) The alternating-nudge unstick is ineffective (21.6% recovery; failing eps thrash ~14 nudges) -> 25% wedged. The collapse RULE is not the culprit (it is calibrated), the episode HORIZON is minor (9.4% clean timeouts), and the post-handoff BASE policy is minor (12.5%).
diagnosis.why_it_is_structural: middle-slow deliberately crawls the CENTER, which is exactly where the reef+skirt sit, so it always faces litter. The robust-ceiling diagnostic policy instead reactively commits to the CLEAN side (99% clean-side, center 0.07) and reaches 0.75-0.86 -- it AVOIDS the litter rather than wading it.
diagnosis.smallest_targeted_intervention: contact-aware speed cut: on detecting litter contact / entering the reef, drop commanded speed to <=0.4 to keep instantaneous planar speed under the 1.2 collapse gate. This directly attacks the 37.5% collapse category. Secondary: replace the alternating-nudge unstick with a more effective recovery (current 21.6%). Both keep the middle strategy.
diagnosis.is_retrain_necessary: Not strictly for a meaningful gain -- the two drivers (speed-burst collapse, weak unstick) are addressable by a small scripted change, likely landing ~0.65-0.75. But the learned adaptive U-blind ceiling is 0.75-0.86 (vs 0.55) by SIDE-committing, so a retrain/adaptive controller captures the full headroom. Retrain is sufficient but not necessary; the minimal scripted fix (contact-aware slowdown) is the smaller first step.
diagnosis.u_asymmetry: coverage u0 0.471 vs u1 0.627. u0 fails via falls(9) + wedged(13); u1 fails via collapse(15). Different failure texture per side.

resim_note: re-sim outcome-faithful for 134/143 coverage eps; 9 borderline eps [304,324,447,531,539,867,938,1171,1342] diverged cross-process (JAX/BLAS float non-determinism over 700-step chaotic rollouts) -> their FALL flag is unreliable and never used to assign the fall category. All other fields are sidecar-authoritative. GIF 3_wedged ep531 is one such unreliable re-sim.
